action: When Engine.Launch dies in the validate gauntlet (go/internal/bridge/launch.go LaunchArgs: missingRequired / LoadProfile / prompt guards → ExitBadFlags=10), the human-readable diagnostic goes to the launch stderr writer but is NOT persisted for in-process orchestrator dispatches — the failure precedes per-agent log-file creation, so the run dir gets no <agent>-stderr.log and failure-diag.json records only the bare exit code. Cycle-270's debugger death surfaced as error_message='debugger: bridge: bridge: launch exit=10' (debugger-failure-diag.json) with zero cause; root-causing took an interactive forensic session (missing .evolve/profiles/debugger.json — fixed 52866168, reproducible via `evolve bridge launch --profile <missing>` → '[bridge] bridge:profile: file not found' rc=10). With the message persisted it would have been one Read. Fix direction: persist validate-stage stderr into the run dir (e.g. <agent>-launch-error.txt) or thread the validation error string into the bridge error chain so failure-diag.json error_message carries it. Acceptance: a launch failing at LoadProfile leaves the cause string in the run dir; <phase>-failure-diag.json error_message includes the '[bridge] …' line, not just exit=10. UPDATE 2026-06-10T18:30Z: cycle-277 audit FAILed on exactly this defect (engine.go:380 stderrBuf captures Deps.Stderr driver diagnostics, NOT the CmdRunner subprocess stderr — architecturally separate buffers; see cycle-277 audit-report failure block). A ready-made failing repro test (TestLaunchErrorIncludesCapturedStderrCause) leaked from cycle-277's bug-reproduction phase and is preserved at .evolve/runs/cycle-277/leaked-launch_error_repro_test.go.txt — reinstate it as the RED test when fixing.
evidence_pointer: .evolve/runs/cycle-270/debugger-failure-diag.json (bare exit=10, duration_ms=0); go/internal/bridge/launch.go:29-61 (every ExitBadFlags path writes stderr first); go/internal/bridge/engine.go:255 (in-process mapping)
